mechanism_narrative: Sustained increases in industrial production (INDPRO) indicate expanding output and favorable business conditions, which drive higher corporate earnings and equity valuations. This positive trend consistently predicts higher S&P 500 forward returns regardless of regime.
failure_condition: If over any 3-year rolling window, months with rising INDPRO (month-over-month) produce lower median forward 60-day returns than months with falling INDPRO, the hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: The unemployment rate (UNRATE) above 6% signals significant labor market slack, reducing disposable income and consumer spending, which depresses corporate profits and leads to negative equity market returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If when UNRATE exceeds 6%, the median direction_20d is not negative (or rank IC is not negative) over a 5-year backtest, reject.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent strength in housing starts (HOUST) and retail sales (RSAFS) provides a reliable signal of robust consumer demand and economic momentum, which amplifies positive equity returns over a quarterly horizon, as both series reflect different dimensions of demand.
failure_condition: If in periods when both HOUST>800 and RSAFS>424000, the forward 60-day return is not positive on average (or more than 60% of instances are positive), then the interaction mechanism fails.

mechanism_narrative: In a zero interest rate policy regime (FEDFUNDS below 0.25%), a rise in the federal funds rate prematurely signals the end of emergency accommodation, increasing discount rates and compressing equity valuations, leading to negative forward returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises above 0.25% within one quarter
failure_condition: If, during ZIRP periods (FEDFUNDS<0.25%), instances where FEDFUNDS rises above 0.25% produce median 20-day returns that are not negative (or not statistically significantly different from zero at 95% confidence), reject.

mechanism_narrative: Growing retail and food services sales (RSAFS) reflect increasing consumer spending, which directly boosts corporate revenues and profits, leading to positive short-term equity market returns over the following week, irrespective of other conditions.
failure_condition: If months with month-over-month increase in RSAFS show median forward 5-day returns equal to or lower than months with decrease, the always-on consumer spending trend signal is invalidated.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating inflation as measured by CPIAUCSL year-over-year growth above a 2% threshold erodes purchasing power and forces the central bank to tighten monetary policy, which negatively impacts equity prices over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: If when CPIAUCSL y/y growth exceeds 2%, the forward 20-day SPX return median is not negative or rank IC is above 0, reject the inflation-signal hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: The joint increase in both payroll employment (PAYEMS) and retail sales (RSAFS) provides a robust confirmation of labor-driven consumption growth, reinforcing corporate earnings expectations and leading to positive equity returns over a 20-day horizon beyond what either series alone predicts.
failure_condition: If the joint signal (PAYEMS>136000 and RSAFS>424000) does not produce significantly higher median forward 20-day returns compared to instances where only one of the proxies meets its threshold (using t-test at 5% level), the interaction hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: During economic recessions (defined as two consecutive quarters of declining real GDP), a positive surprise in retail sales (RSAFS) signals a potential end to the downturn and an imminent recovery, generating strong positive equity returns over the subsequent quarter as investors anticipate improving demand.
failure_condition: If in recession periods (GDPC1 declining for 2 quarters), months with positive RSAFS growth produce median forward 60-day returns not exceeding non-recession periods with positive RSAFS growth, or if the median is not positive, the regime-conditional signal fails.
